Xiamen Zhongyang Automotive Industry Co., Ltd. presents a profile of an early 1990s legacy industrial enterprise that has undergone a dramatic structural pivot. However, forensic analysis of the corrupted data reveals a corrected workforce of 0 insured employees (2024). Furthermore, its extracted business scope—while retaining legacy 'manufacturing' licenses—heavily emphasizes 'New Energy Vehicle (NEV) sales, charging piles, battery swapping facilities' and crucially, 'Real Estate and Housing Leasing'. The combination of zero employees, a prime urban location in Siming District, and an extreme pivot from heavy manufacturing to NEV retail and property leasing concludes that this entity has completely ceased physical manufacturing. It operates today as an asset-heavy holding shell, likely utilizing its valuable legacy industrial land for auto dealership parks or real estate rentals.
